results.reason: The settlement is enclosed by a wall, which serves as a natural boundary.
results.title: Bounded perimeter

results.reason: The paths do not branch and curve around buildings with varied width. They are more uniform in their layout.
results.title: Organic branching paths

results.reason: Path rhythm and door alignment: Paths have compression/release and bends, but they do not meet building fronts at their doors. Instead, they dead-end against walls.
results.title: Path rhythm and door alignment

results.reason: Building footprints differ in massing, but the variety is limited to a few distinct styles.
results.title: Varied building massing

results.reason: The place has a single re-tinted material over flat ground. There is no real contrast in materials used.
results.title: Distinct material identity

results.reason: Decoration scatters through the walkable interior, but it does not cluster at walls, path edges, and pockets. Instead, it is more sparse.
results.title: Decoration density

results.reason: There is no dominant landmark that clearly dominates the first read; the eye does not have something specific to move toward.
results.title: Dominant landmark

results.reason: The first view does not immediately say what kind of place this is and does not promise something beyond the current screen.
results.title: Readable first view

results.reason: Reward / curiosity objects read as lone unstyled markers in empty space, rather than dressed reward clusters.
results.title: Dressed reward clusters

results.reason: There is no landmark that uses water (pond, fountain, river) or the open square is organically shaped rather than a hard rectangle. The layout is more rigid.
results.title: Water or organic plaza

results.reason: A limited, consistent material vocabulary is reused throughout, but it does not introduce unrelated materials in each area.
results.title: Limited coherent vocabulary

results.reason: backend did not address this criterion
results.title: Memorable identity
